headline: Stronger China Quarter And Turnaround Progress Could Be A Game Changer For Nike (NKE)
summary: Nike recently reported fiscal third quarter results that exceeded analyst expectations on both revenue and earnings, with Nike brand revenue reaching US$11.00 billion and China profitability outpacing forecasts despite ongoing softness in Converse and margin pressure. Management framed the quarter as further, but incomplete, progress in a broad turnaround under CEO Elliott Hill, highlighting actions to improve business “health and quality” alongside higher-profile initiatives such as the...

headline: Nike Shares Drop Nearly 9% in After-Hours Trading; Sales Outlook Is Weak
summary: Nike shares fell nearly 9% in after-hours trading after the sneaker company said its revenue for the current quarter would slip between 2% and 4%, driven down by a sharp decline in China sales.  The sneaker company reported $11.3 billion in revenue for the quarter ended Feb. 28, a total that beat Wall Street expectations though it was flat from a year earlier.  “Parts of it are taking longer than I'd like, but the direction is clear,” Hill said of the turnaround.

headline: Nike Q3 earnings beat estimates, with China business in focus
summary: Nike (NKE) reported third quarter results on Tuesday after the closing bell. Earnings per share (EPS) came in at $0.35 (compared to analyst estimates of $0.31), and revenue came in at $11.28 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $11.24 billion).Earnings in China before interest and taxes came in at $467 million, compared to estimates of $269.5 million.Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton takes a closer look at the breaking numbers.
